title: Bond Trading Overview (Daily)
subtitle: 上海证券交易所 · 债券成交概况（日度）
description:
This page displays daily bond trading statistics for the Shanghai Stock Exchange.
    Data is loaded dynamically via a JS object — the visible table is populated by an internal AJAX call
    after a date is selected. The underlying API is open and can be called directly.
data_desc: Agent 可以从这个页面获取什么数据，以及怎样获取。

functions.label: Internal API
functions.description:
页面前端日期选择器的底层数据源。公开接口，无鉴权、无 CORS 限制。
        返回指定日期的全部债券品种成交数据。

params.name: searchDate
params.desc: 查询日期。格式 YYYY-MM-DD，如 2025-02-11

params.name: jsonCallBack
params.desc: JSONP 回调名。省略则返回纯 JSON。

extra_sections.label: Bond Types (Rows)
extra_sections.content: 国债 · 地方政府债 · 企业债 · 公司债 · 可转换债 · 可分离债 · 资产支持证券 · 国际机构债 · 政策性金融债 · 同业存单 · ... (17 categories total)

caveats: Non-trading days (weekends, holidays) return all-zero rows
Data availability: T+1, updated ~1 hour after market close (15:30 CST)
Overseas IP may get 403 — use a CN-based proxy if needed

internals.desc: 如果你需要通过浏览器自动化操作此页面（而非直调 API），以下是页面的内部结构。

kv.value: overviewDay.setOverviewDayParams() — 读取输入框日期并发起 AJAX 请求刷新表格
.js_date input — 日期输入框，设置 .value = 'YYYY-MM-DD' 后调用 trigger method
.table-responsive table — 标准 thead/tbody 结构，17 行 × 4 列
Server-side HTML shell + client-side JS data injection. 日期切换通过 AJAX 异步加载，DOM 直接更新。
None — 公开数据，无登录、无 token、无 CSRF
None detected — 无 rate limit、无 captcha、无 IP 封禁（CN 内网）
非交易日判定：表格所有数值为 0、0.00、-- 或空字符串

internals.recipe_desc: 如果直调 API 受阻，以下 action steps 可通过 Playwright/Puppeteer 复现数据获取：
internals.automation_recipe: // Step 1: Set date document.querySelector('.js_date input').value = '2025-02-11' // Step 2: Trigger query (wait 3s for AJAX response) overviewDay.setOverviewDayParams() // Step 3: Extract table const table = document.querySelector('.table-responsive table') const headers = [...table.querySelectorAll('thead th')].map(th => th.textContent.trim()) const rows = [...table.querySelectorAll('tbody tr')].map(tr => [...tr.querySelectorAll('td')].map(td => td.textContent.trim()) )

reports_desc: 使用过此数据源的 Agent 自动提交的调用记录与注意事项。

reports.body: 直调 API，searchDate=2025-02-28，成功返回 17 行 JSON。国债成交额 1,892.45 亿元。
批量查询 2025-01-02 ~ 2025-01-31。23 个交易日全部成功。注意：2024-12-31 虽为交易日但数据偏低（半日交易）。
从 US 服务器直调 API 返回 403 Forbidden。改用中国境内代理后正常。建议海外 Agent 注意 IP 限制。

title: Cryptocurrency Market Data (Live)
subtitle: CoinPaprika · Bitcoin Ticker
description:
CoinPaprika provides free cryptocurrency market data via a public REST API.
    The Bitcoin ticker endpoint returns comprehensive price, market cap, volume, and
    historical change data in a single request — no API key required.

functions.label: Public API
functions.description:
Returns comprehensive ticker data for Bitcoin including price, market cap,
        volume, circulating/total/max supply, and percentage changes across multiple timeframes.

params.name: quotes
params.desc: Comma-separated list of quote currencies. Default: USD. Options: USD, BTC, ETH, etc.

functions.returns_desc: JSON object with the following key fields:
caveats: Data is delayed approximately 5 minutes from real-time
Prices are in USD by default; use quotes param for other currencies
Free tier: no rate limit published, but recommended < 10 req/min
No CORS restrictions — callable from anywhere globally

internals.desc: How the website works internally, for browser automation fallback.

kv.value: window.__NEXT_DATA__ — server-rendered JSON props embedded in the page
.cp-coin-header .price — main price element, updated via client-side React state
.cp-stat-box — contains market cap, volume, supply, ATH, and other metrics
Server-side rendered HTML with React hydration. Data available immediately in __NEXT_DATA__ without waiting for client JS.
None — public page, no login or token required
None detected — no CORS restrictions, no rate limiting, no captcha
Use the REST API directly — no browser automation needed

internals.recipe_desc: The REST API is the recommended approach. If you still need browser data:
internals.automation_recipe: // Option 1: Extract from __NEXT_DATA__ (fastest) const data = JSON.parse( document.querySelector('#__NEXT_DATA__').textContent ) const ticker = data.props.pageProps.ticker // Option 2: Extract from DOM const price = document.querySelector('.cp-coin-header .price')?.textContent const stats = [...document.querySelectorAll('.cp-stat-box')].map(el => el.textContent.trim())

reports.body: Called GET /v1/tickers/btc-bitcoin successfully. BTC price $97,234.56, market cap $1.92T. Response is a single JSON object with nested quotes — straightforward to parse.

Batch-queried top 20 coins by replacing coin_id in path: /v1/tickers/eth-ethereum, /v1/tickers/usdt-tether, etc. All 20 returned successfully. Alternatively, use /v1/tickers for all coins at once (larger payload).

title: Weather Forecast API
subtitle: Open-Meteo · Free Weather API
description:
Open-Meteo provides a free, open-source weather forecast API with no API key required.
    Supports current conditions, hourly/daily forecasts, and historical data for any location
    on Earth via latitude/longitude coordinates.

functions.label: Public API
functions.description:
Returns weather forecast data for a given location. Supports current conditions,
        hourly forecasts (up to 16 days), and daily aggregations. Highly configurable via query parameters.

params.name: latitude
params.desc: Latitude of the location. E.g. 39.9042 (Beijing)

params.name: longitude
params.desc: Longitude of the location. E.g. 116.4074 (Beijing)

params.name: timezone
params.desc: Timezone name. E.g. Asia/Shanghai, auto

functions.returns_desc: JSON object with current conditions and time-series arrays:
caveats: Free tier: 10,000 requests/day, non-commercial use
Must specify at least one of current, hourly, or daily
No CORS restrictions — callable from anywhere globally
Weather codes follow WMO standard (see docs for mapping)

internals.desc: How the website works internally.

kv.value: Documentation page — no dynamic data tables on the website itself
REST API at api.open-meteo.com — the website is documentation, not a data viewer
Stateless GET requests with query parameters. No session, no cookies, no client-side state.
None — no API key, no registration, no token
10,000 req/day on free tier. No per-minute limit documented.
API-first — no browser automation needed. Call the REST API directly.

internals.recipe_desc: This is an API-first service. No browser automation recipe is needed — simply construct the query URL with desired parameters.
internals.automation_recipe: // Get 7-day forecast for New York const url = `https://api.open-meteo.com/v1/forecast?` + `latitude=40.7128&longitude=-74.0060` + `&daily=temperature_2m_max,temperature_2m_min,weather_code` + `&timezone=America/New_York&forecast_days=7` const res = await fetch(url) const data = await res.json() // data.daily.time = ["2025-03-01", "2025-03-02", ...] // data.daily.temperature_2m_max = [12.5, 14.1, ...] // data.daily.temperature_2m_min = [3.2, 5.0, ...]

reports.body: Queried Beijing weather (39.9042, 116.4074) with current + 7-day daily forecast. Temperature 12.3°C, clear sky (weather_code=0). Response well-structured with parallel arrays for time and values.

Batch-queried 5 cities (Beijing, Tokyo, NYC, London, Sydney) for 7-day forecasts. All 5 returned successfully. Note: response uses parallel arrays (time[], temp[]) — zip them together by index for per-day data.

title: Hacker News — Top Stories
subtitle: Firebase API · Y Combinator
description:
Hacker News provides a public Firebase-based API for accessing top stories, new stories,
    and individual items. The API uses a two-step pattern: first fetch a list of story IDs,
    then fetch each item individually. No authentication required.

functions.label: Function 1 of 2
functions.description:
Returns an array of up to 500 item IDs for the current top stories on Hacker News,
        ordered by ranking. Use these IDs to fetch individual story details via Function 2.
functions.returns_desc: JSON array of up to 500 integer item IDs:

functions.label: Function 2 of 2
functions.description:
Returns the full details of a single Hacker News item (story, comment, job, poll, etc.).
        Use the IDs from Function 1 to fetch individual stories.

response_fields.desc: External URL (may be absent for Ask HN)
Item type: story, comment, job, poll

caveats: Two-step pattern: first get IDs, then fetch each item individually
For batch fetching, use concurrent requests (e.g. Promise.all with top 10 IDs)
Some stories may not have a url field (Ask HN, Show HN text posts)
No rate limit documented, but be respectful — recommended < 30 req/sec
No CORS restrictions — callable from anywhere globally

internals.desc: How the website works internally, for browser automation fallback.

kv.value: Server-side rendered HTML tables — no JavaScript framework, no client-side rendering
tr.athing — each story is a table row with class "athing"
.titleline a — the story title link within each row
.score — the score element in the subtext row (next sibling of tr.athing)
None — pure static HTML, no client-side JavaScript controllers
None — public page, no login required for reading
None detected — no rate limiting, no captcha on the page

internals.recipe_desc: If you prefer extracting directly from the HTML page:
internals.automation_recipe: // Extract top stories from the HTML page const stories = [...document.querySelectorAll('tr.athing')].map(row => { const titleEl = row.querySelector('.titleline a') const subtext = row.nextElementSibling const scoreEl = subtext?.querySelector('.score') return { id: row.getAttribute('id'), title: titleEl?.textContent, url: titleEl?.getAttribute('href'), score: parseInt(scoreEl?.textContent) || 0 } })

reports.body: Fetched top 10 stories via two-step API: topstories.json → slice(0,10) → Promise.all for items. All 10 returned successfully. Top story: "Show HN: ..." with 342 points.
Batch-fetched all 500 top story IDs, then concurrently fetched items in batches of 50. Total time ~8s for 500 stories. Note: some items return null (deleted stories) — filter these out.
